affected_groups: children in the current SEN system
content.decision_question: Should Hungary maintain a deliberate no-change baseline to generate clean counterfactual evidence, or treat continued drift under known structural and legal pressures as itself an unacceptable policy choice requiring immediate intervention?
content.evidence_boundary: Trend data can show whether identification, capacity strain, and legal exposure are worsening under status quo conditions, informing urgency assessments. It cannot resolve whether the value of methodological rigor (a clean counterfactual) should be weighed against the value of harm prevention, since that tradeoff is a normative one.
content.tension: A passive baseline (TP-live-t1) is proposed as a rigorous counterfactual to isolate the effects of demographic and system drift, but multiple lenses argue that inaction is not neutral because it allows compounding legal exposure, capacity strain, and sorting mechanisms to continue unaddressed, raising the question of whether deliberate non-intervention is a legitimate policy stance or an implicit acceptance of continued harm.
content.title: Passive baseline: analytical counterfactual versus de facto policy of drift
value_poles: obligation to act on known, worsening harms
